title: 6. Example using Turbinaria
text: Fractional membership means sample represented mixture topics rather assigned one topic. Instead saying: sample belongs topic 2 LDA says something like: sample 70% topic 2, 20% topic 5, 10% topic 1 topic proportions sum 1 sample. useful samples contain mixed communities, transitional states, multiple co-occurring assemblages. dominant topic topic largest proportion sample. example, sample 80% topic 4, topic 4 dominant topic. maximum topic proportion describes strongly sample dominated one topic. value near 1 means sample nearly pure. lower value means sample mixed. number topics 5% gives simple count many topics meaningfully contribute sample. sample one topic 5%, can treated mixed sample. entropy summarises evenly spread sample across topics. Low entropy means one topic dominates. text: core method metric. partitions SymPortal profiles three mutually exclusive classes don’t contaminate one another: confident -splits, mixtures, orphans. Report mixture claim reproducible profiles (n >= 2); fold n = 1 profiles orphan/uncertain category.

title: 1. SymBayes Overview
text: SymPortal assigns ITS2 amplicon data type profiles using deterministic co-occurrence rules. two well-known failure modes: -splits (creating many near-identical profiles differ minor intragenomic variants), mistakes mixed symbiont communities unique types (giving admixed sample single compound profile name). symbayes reframes profiling probabilistic mixture modelling provides three complementary models, answering different question:

title: 1. SymBayes Overview
text: Everything flows single object created import() function imports sections Symportal output: data imported sp, fit_*() adds model sp$dmm, sp$lda, sp$hdp, sharing one schema, generalist plots work .
code: library(symbayes)  sp <- import(   seqs_abund  = "run.seqs.absolute.abund_only.txt",   profs_abund = "run.profiles.absolute.abund_only.txt",   profs_meta  = "run.profiles.meta_only.txt",   colour_dict = "color_dict_post_med.json" ) sp <- filter_samples(sp, min_reads = 100, min_prev = 2) sp <- fit_dmm(sp)          # sp$dmm sp <- fit_lda(sp)          # sp$lda  (adaptive K) sp <- fit_hdp(sp)          # sp$hdp  (threshold-free)  sp                          # print method summarises fitted models

title: Threshold-free, mixture-aware Symbiodiniaceae ITS2 profiling
text: Threshold-free, mixture-aware profiling Symbiodiniaceae ITS2 amplicon data — probabilistic alternative deterministic type profiles. symbayes reframes profiling probabilistic mixture modelling determine intragenomic variants admixtures using Dirichlet-Multinomial Mixture, Latent Dirichlet Allocation, Hierarchical Dirichlet Process models.

title: Threshold-free, mixture-aware Symbiodiniaceae ITS2 profiling
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("marine-ecologist/symbayes")  # HDP support remotes::install_github("NickWilliamsSanger/hdp")  # DirichletMultinomial via Bioconductor: # BiocManager::install("DirichletMultinomial")

title: Threshold-free, mixture-aware Symbiodiniaceae ITS2 profiling
code: library(symbayes)  sp <- import(   seqs_abund  = "run.seqs.absolute.abund_only.txt",   profs_abund = "run.profiles.absolute.abund_only.txt",   profs_meta  = "run.profiles.meta_only.txt",   colour_dict = "color_dict_post_med.json" ) sp <- filter_samples(sp, min_reads = 100, min_prev = 2)  sp <- fit_dmm(sp)     # how many discrete types?      (hard assignment) sp <- fit_lda(sp)     # what is each sample made of?   (fractional, adaptive K) sp <- fit_hdp(sp)     # both, threshold-free           (K inferred)  compare_models(sp) hdp_vs_symportal(sp)  # over-splitting quantification

title: Compare a soft (fractional) model against SymPortal's profiles via the fuzzy Rand index — compare_soft_hard
text: pair samples probability co-assigned dot product membership vectors (theta %*% t(theta)). SymPortal's multi-profile proportions treated way, giving two soft co-membership matrices. fuzzy Rand index 1 - mean|EQ_model - EQ_sp| pairs; adjusted version corrects chance agreement (raw index inflated many pairs clearly separate ). Also returns per-sample co-membership divergence (two methods disagree — typically admixed samples) disagreement breakdown separating "SymPortal missed mixing model found" "SymPortal invented compound profile model sees pure".

what: Fit a Hierarchical Dirichlet Process — fit_hdp
title: Fit a Hierarchical Dirichlet Process — fit_hdp
text: threshold-free, mixture-aware core symbayes. Unlike DMM LDA, number community types inferred data rather specified: DP concentration parameters (Gamma hyperpriors) govern component number, samples may admixed. Uses hdp package (Roberts). See https://github.com/nicolaroberts/hdp.

title: Fit a Hierarchical Dirichlet Process — fit_hdp
code: fit_hdp(   sp,   group_var = NULL,   n_chains = 4,   burnin = 5000,   n = 100,   space = 50,   initcc = 10,   alphaa = 1,   alphab = 1,   cos_merge = 0.9,   min_sample = 1,   seed = 42 )

title: Fit a Hierarchical Dirichlet Process — fit_hdp
text: sp filtered symbayes object. group_var Optional metadata column defining middle DP layer, NULL (default) flat, covariate-free structure. n_chains Independent Gibbs chains (default 4). burnin Burn-iterations per chain (default 5000). n Posterior samples collected per chain (default 100). space Iterations collected samples (default 50). initcc Initial cluster count (default 10; starting point ). alphaa, alphab Gamma hyperprior (shape, rate) DP concentration (default 1, 1). cos_merge Cosine similarity raw clusters merged extraction (default 0.90; hdp default). Higher retains components. set low everything collapses. min_sample Minimum posterior samples component must appear retained (default 1). seed Base random seed (chains use seed + chain).

title: Fit a Hierarchical Dirichlet Process — fit_hdp
text: symbayes object sp$hdp populated.

title: Fit a Hierarchical Dirichlet Process — fit_hdp
text: group_var = NULL structure flat (one parent DP, one child DP per sample) assignment covariate-free. Supplying metadata column (e.g. "host_species") inserts middle DP layer samples within group share strength preferentially. Caution: grouping covariate later want test (e.g. host effect symbiont community) introduces circularity; use flat structure host-blind claims, grouped explicitly-labelled predictive enhancement. Writes membership object sp$hdp. theta excludes residual (unassigned) mass, kept meta$residual. meta also holds entropy, max_prop, n_comp per sample, raw_ncl (sampler cluster count), multi chain object.

title: Fit a Hierarchical Dirichlet Process — fit_hdp

title: Fit a Latent Dirichlet Allocation topic model — fit_lda

title: Quantify SymPortal over-classification relative to HDP — hdp_vs_symportal
text: core method metric. Compares SymPortal's profile count HDP's inferred component count , crucially, partitions SymPortal profiles three mutually exclusive classes contaminate one another:

title: Quantify SymPortal over-classification relative to HDP — hdp_vs_symportal
Quantify SymPortal over-classification relative to HDP — hdp_vs_symportal
text: sp symbayes object HDP fitted SymPortal profiles present. over_split_min_frac Minimum dominant-component share profile count -split (default 0.7). mixture_thresh Dominant-component share profile mixture (default 0.7).

title: Quantify SymPortal over-classification relative to HDP — hdp_vs_symportal
text: Invisibly, list n_sp_profiles, n_hdp_comp, oversplit_ratio, mapping, redundant_splits, mixture_profiles (profile-level), mixture_samples (one row per sample whose dominant profile mixture, ranked comp_i/frac_i columns showing HDP components co-occur individual sample), orphan_profiles.

title: Quantify SymPortal over-classification relative to HDP — hdp_vs_symportal
text: -splitting Several profiles confidently map one HDP component (dominant_frac >= over_split_min_frac). SymPortal split one real community type multiple named profiles. Mixtures Profiles whose samples spread across components (dominant_frac < mixture_thresh). SymPortal called admixture unique type; force-assigned. Orphans confidently component clear mixtures (usually rare, n = 1). Reported separately excluded -splitting count. partition matters: rare profile whose single sample largest component percent must counted "-split " component.

what: symbayes: Threshold-Free, Mixture-Aware Symbiodiniaceae ITS2 Profiling — symbayes-package
title: symbayes: Threshold-Free, Mixture-Aware Symbiodiniaceae ITS2 Profiling — symbayes-package
text: symbayes provides probabilistic alternative SymPortal's deterministic ITS2 type profiles. reads SymPortal output fits three complementary mixture models, answering different question:

title: symbayes: Threshold-Free, Mixture-Aware Symbiodiniaceae ITS2 Profiling — symbayes-package
text: DMM (fit_dmm) many discrete community types dataset support? Dirichlet-Multinomial Mixture formal model selection (Laplace/BIC). Hard assignment: sample maps one component. LDA (fit_lda) sample made ? Latent Dirichlet Allocation redundancy-based topic-number selection. Fractional membership: sample can mixture topics. HDP (fit_hdp) Threshold-free mixture-aware: number community types inferred data via Hierarchical Dirichlet Process, samples may admixed.

what: The sp object and the membership schema
title: symbayes: Threshold-Free, Mixture-Aware Symbiodiniaceae ITS2 Profiling — symbayes-package
text: Every function takes returns single list class symbayes (created import). Fitting model writes standardised membership object sp$dmm, sp$lda, sp$hdp, schema: theta samples x groups membership matrix (near one-hot DMM, fractional LDA/HDP) beta groups x sequences exemplar compositions dominant per-sample dominant group (factor) k number groups meta model-specific extras (certainty, entropy, residual, ...) three models expose schema, generalist plotting functions (plot_pcoa, plot_barplot, plot_exemplars, plot_contingency, plot_membership) work fitted model via model = c("dmm", "lda", "hdp") argument.

title: symbayes: Threshold-Free, Mixture-Aware Symbiodiniaceae ITS2 Profiling — symbayes-package
